mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) like Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It simplifies the deployment and management of AI models in Kubernetes environments, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and the complexity of managing model versions.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and configured.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command retrieves the credentials for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama in its own namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> This command uninstalls the Ollama release.
> This command deletes the Ollama namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process involves upgrading the Ollama Helm release and verifying the deployment afterward.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Drain the node to safely upgrade.
> Upgrade the Ollama release.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Ollama image is not available or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure the image exists on Docker Hub.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama pod is failing to start, possibly due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for Ollama.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes API server unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a connectivity issue between your local environment and the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up and that the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5, improved API response times.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n ollama'. All pods should be in a 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
